channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran fires missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain, U.S. strikes Iran facility
item.description: The U.S. military said that Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain that failed or were shot down, and that the U.S. launched strikes on an Iranian facility in response.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military said that Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain that failed or were shot down, and that the U.S. launched strikes on an Iranian facility in response.


(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Scenes from the aftermath of Russia's latest massive attack on Kyiv
item.description: Russian missile and drone attacks killed civilians across Ukraine overnight, in one of Moscow's largest assaults in recent months.
item.content:encoded:
Russian missile and drone attacks killed civilians across Ukraine overnight, in one of Moscow's largest assaults in recent months.

(Image credit: Anton Shtuka for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Polina Lytvynova

item.title: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad
item.description: The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.
item.content:encoded:
The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.

(Image credit: Jean-Francois Badias)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Global conflicts push Sri Lanka's economy to the brink
item.description: Sri Lanka was careening from crisis to crisis, then came the Mideast war. People say they've got no buffer left.
item.content:encoded: Sri Lanka was careening from crisis to crisis, then came the Mideast war. People say they've got no buffer left.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters block roads and trains across Israel over military draft
item.description: Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.
item.content:encoded:
Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.

(Image credit: Ohad Zwigenberg)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What to know about U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats
item.description: The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Russian attack on Ukraine kills 22 people, officials say, as Moscow escalates fighting
item.description: Russian forces launched a massive aerial attack on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, and officials say at least 22 civilians were killed and 138 were wounded. The assault began overnight and continued during the day.
item.content:encoded:
Russian forces launched a massive aerial attack on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, and officials say at least 22 civilians were killed and 138 were wounded. The assault began overnight and continued during the day.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran halts talks with U.S. over Israeli actions in Lebanon, Gaza
item.description: Trumps says Israel and Hezbollah will stop fighting after Iran stops talks with U.S. over Israeli operations in southern Lebanon, Gaza
item.content:encoded:
Trumps says Israel and Hezbollah will stop fighting after Iran stops talks with U.S. over Israeli operations in southern Lebanon, Gaza

(Image credit: STR/AP Photo/undefined)
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: U.S. strikes Iranian military sites. And, Trump's $1.8 billion fund faces scrutiny
item.description: American aircraft fired on a number of Iranian sites over the weekend, including Qeeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz. And, Trump's anti-weaponization fund faces scrutiny in Congress and the courts.
item.content:encoded:
American aircraft fired on a number of Iranian sites over the weekend, including Qeeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz. And, Trump's anti-weaponization fund faces scrutiny in Congress and the courts.

(Image credit: Kawnat Haju)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo near 300 as survivors describe their recoveries
item.description: The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.
item.content:encoded:
The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
